rulings.Case Title: RICHARD A DENTON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Richard Denton's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. While it may turn out that Mr. Denton's claims against Barbara Hale and Lori Mitchell and his new factual allegations are time-barred and/or lack merit, the record on this motion does not establish as a matter of law that Mr. Denton cannot prevail on any claims against Ms. Hale and Ms. Mitchell or on any of his new factual allegations. Nor have defendants CCSF and John Doyle shown that they will be legally prejudiced by allowing Mr. Denton to make his new factual allegations against them. Under these circumstances, especially since no trial date has been set, California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings requires the granting of this motion. Mr. Denton has leave to file his second amended complaint by May 24, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Denton is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SATINDER SINGH VS. XUONG LU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT BALWANT GILL'S Notice Of Motion For Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Balwant Gill's motion for good faith settlement is granted. The evidence demonstrates that Mr. Gill has little, if any, liability for the accident. (Barrack Dec., Ex. A, Snook Depo. 44:19-47:10.) The most compelling evidence-the video-shows that plaintiff Satinder Singh's vehicle was pushed into the lane where Mr. Gill was driving, Mr. Gill moved to the shoulder to avoid Mr. Singh's vehicle, and Mr. Gill made slight contact with Mr. Singh's vehicle. (Barrack Dec., Ex. B.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that Mr. Gill has no liability. The settlement is therefore in the "ballpark" even though Mr. Singh's claimed damages are currently unsettled. There is substantial evidence that the settlement was made in good faith. "[P]ractical considerations obviously require that the evaluation be made on the basis of information available at the time of settlement." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Gill is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO GUACAMAYA GARCIA VS. CHAMPION STEAM CLEANING, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiff To Provide Contact Information For Court Reporter Of Deposition Of Shen Bao Huang And To Pay Expenses For Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jeffrey A. Needleman W/Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT SHENBAO HUANG'S Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiff To Provide Contact Information For Court Reporter Of Deposition Of Shen Bao Huang And To Pay Expenses For Motion. Defendant Shen Bao Huang's motion for an order requiring plaintiff to provide contact information for court reporter of his deposition is denied as moot and both sides' requests for CCP 128.5 monetary sanctions are denied. Mr. Huang had been provided with the information he sought to receive by his motion prior to his filing the motion, so that part of the motion is moot. While both sides engaged in behavior that falls below what is expected from cooperative and civil attorneys, neither side acted in a way that can fairly be characterized as in bad faith, frivolous or solely for the purpose of causing difficulty or delay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MANMEET SARANG VS. GARY MITCHELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions For Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT GARY MITCHELL, TRICIA MARTINEZ's Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions For Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition is filed. It appears from the file that the plaintiff has been nonresponsive to his counsel for many months. The motion for terminating sanctions is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE ASTABIE VS. LORENC KUQI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Seal, Remove, Or Delete Previously Filed Inital Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT KRIZTINA EMODI Motion To Seal, Remove, Or Delete Previously Filed Inital Complaint. Defendant Krisztina Emodi's motion to seal, remove, or delete previously filed initial complaint is denied. Ms. Emodi fails to show an overriding interest that trumps the presumptive right of public access to all court documents, especially since the document she seeks to make unavailable to the public has been publicly available for approximately a year and a half. If Ms. Emodi's showing was sufficient, then all prevailing defendants who assert an actual or potential collateral consequence from having been sued would be entitled to have the complaints against them sealed. This is far beyond what the sealing rules contemplate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY DUNCAN VS. FLUENT LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTS MOCADE MEDICA LLC, RGO MEDIA LLC, ART BROCK HOLDINGS LLC, CGIW MARKETING SERVICES LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Mocade Media LLC, RGO Media LLC, Art Brock Holdings LLC, and CGIW Marketing Services LLC's motion to strike the first amended complaint is denied. A motion to dismiss, not a motion to strike, is the proper procedural vehicle to raise the issue of untimely service and what sanction is appropriate. On a motion to strike the court entertains evidence (e.g. declarations regarding why service is delayed) to determine whether a dismissal is warranted because of untimely service. A motion to strike is a "non-speaking" motion that relies on the allegations in the complaint, not evidence. Moving defendants do not highlight anything in the first amended complaint that renders it defective under the motion to strike statute. Moving defendants' statute of limitations argument lacks merit. The date of service of a complaint is an issue distinct from whether the plaintiff commenced the action within the statute of limitations. Moving defendants fail to explain why the relation back doctrine does not apply. Any issue regarding sanctions for untimely service should be raised with department 610 and that department can address those issues at the June 19, 2018 OSC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY DUNCAN VS. FLUENT LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT MOCADE MEDICA LLC, RGO MEDIA LLC, ART BROCK HOLDINGS LLC, CGIW MARKETING SERVICES LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Mocade Media LLC, RGO Media LLC, Art Brock Holdings LLC, and CGIW Marketing Services LLC's demurrer to the sole cause of action in the first amended complaint is overruled. A motion to dismiss, not a demurrer, is the proper procedural vehicle to raise the issue of untimely service and what sanction is appropriate. On a moton to dismiss the court entertains evidence (e.g. declarations regarding why service is delayed) to determine whether a dismissal is warranted because of untimely service. A demurrer is a "non-speaking" motion that relies on the allegations in the complaint, not evidence. Demurring defendants do not highlight anything in the first amended complaint that renders it defective under the demurrer statute. Demurring defendants' statute of limitations argument lacks merit. The date of service of a complaint is an issue distinct from whether the plaintiff commenced the action within the statute of limitations. Demurring defendants fail to explain why the relation back doctrine does not apply. Any issue regarding sanctions for untimely service should be raised with department 610 and that department can address those issues at the June 19, 2018 OSC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SEYEDEH KALANTARI VS. BLOOMINGDALE'S INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Discovery Under Ccp2017.220(A)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT BLOOMINGDALE'S INC. Notice Of Motion For Discovery Under CCP 2017.220(A). Defendants Bloomingdale's, Inc. and Andre Lawrence's motion for discovery is denied in its entirety. All of the questions that plaintiff Seyedeh Kalantari was instructed not to answer at her deposition implicate her privacy rights. None of those questions seek sufficiently probative information which outweigh Ms. Kalantrari's privacy. All of the questions seek information that is tangential to the main issues in this case and/or merely corroborative of Mr. Rivera's testimony that he had an intimate relationship with Ms. Kalantari. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Kalantari is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROMULA VILLANUEVA VS. SANTOS A. CADIENTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ROMULA VILLANUEVA'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Romula Villanueva's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Counsel for defendant Santo Cadiente filed a declaration of non-opposition with "conditions" and listed three conditions that he desired. While the first and third conditions do not appear to be controversial, the court is unable to determine either the purpose of the second condition or whether it is opposed. Since no explanation has been provided for why any of the three conditions are requested, the court will leave it to Ms. Villanueva whether to comply with them. Ms. Villanueva must file her first amended complaint no later than May 23, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Villanueva is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DUKE PARTNERS II, LLC VS. KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Duke Partners Ii, Llc Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Line 12, Plaintiff Duke Partners II, LLC'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff Duke Partners II, LLC's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There is a triable dispute whether defendant Keyhan Mohanna is currently in possession of the real property that is the subject of this eviction action. Because the essence of an unlawful detainer action is the recovery of physical possession of real property, Duke Partners' argument that it can proceed on a "constructive possession" theory lacks merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Mohanna is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
